name: Bollywood
description: India's Hindi-language film industry — the superstars, blockbusters, and all-time classics.

questions.q: The name 'Bollywood' is a portmanteau of Hollywood and which Indian city where the Hindi film industry is based?

source.notes: Blend of 'Bombay' (city's former name) and 'Hollywood.'

questions.q: Bollywood produces films primarily in which language?

source.notes: Other regional industries have their own names (Kollywood, Tollywood, etc.).

questions.q: Who is famously known as the 'King of Bollywood' or the 'King of Romance'?

source.notes: Widely known as SRK or 'King Khan.'

questions.q: Which veteran actor, nicknamed 'Big B' or 'Shahenshah,' has dominated Bollywood since the 1970s?

source.notes: Debut film 'Saat Hindustani' (1969); breakthrough 'Zanjeer' (1973).

questions.q: Which Bollywood star married American pop singer Nick Jonas in 2018?

source.notes: Ceremony in Jodhpur, India.

questions.q: Which Academy Award did A.R. Rahman win two of in 2009 for a film about an Indian slum?
choices: Best Original Song and Best Original Score
Best Adapted Screenplay and Best Actor

source.notes: For Slumdog Millionaire, 2008.

questions.q: Which Bollywood film song-and-dance extravaganza routinely runs longer than a typical Hollywood movie?

source.notes: Typical runtime with a mid-film intermission.

questions.q: Which 2009 Aamir Khan film follows three engineering students questioning rote learning?

source.notes: Directed by Rajkumar Hirani.

questions.q: Which director helmed both '3 Idiots' and the 'Munna Bhai' films?

source.notes: Hirani's films are known for blending comedy with social issues.

questions.q: Which 2001 Aamir Khan epic, set during the British Raj, was nominated for Best Foreign Language Film at the Oscars?

source.notes: Directed by Ashutosh Gowariker.

questions.q: Which Bollywood actress made her Hollywood lead debut in the 2017 reboot of 'Baywatch' as Victoria Leeds?

source.notes: Chopra played the villain opposite Dwayne Johnson.

questions.q: Which 2019 Ranveer Singh film by Zoya Akhtar centered on Mumbai's underground rap scene?

source.notes: Inspired by rappers Divine and Naezy.

questions.q: Which Bollywood actress is the daughter of late superstar Sridevi and producer Boney Kapoor?

source.notes: Janhvi debuted in 2018's 'Dhadak.'

questions.q: The 2016 Aamir Khan sports drama about wrestling father Mahavir Phogat and his daughters is called what?

source.notes: Highest-grossing Indian film at its release.

questions.q: Which three actresses were considered the legendary 'Kapoor sisters' trio of the 1990s-2000s in Bollywood?

source.notes: Daughters of Randhir Kapoor and Babita.

questions.q: Which legendary playback singer known as the 'Nightingale of India' died in 2022 after a career of over 70 years?

source.notes: Recorded in dozens of Indian languages.

questions.q: Which film is generally considered India's first 'talkie' — the first Hindi-language sound film?

source.notes: Released 14 March 1931.

questions.q: Which actor is known as the 'Dabangg' series star and 'Bhaijaan' of Bollywood?

source.notes: Dabangg (2010) and sequels.

questions.q: Who is traditionally called the 'father of Indian cinema' for directing the 1913 silent film 'Raja Harishchandra'?

source.notes: India's highest film-industry award is named for Phalke.

questions.q: Who was the first Indian to win an Academy Award, for Best Costume Design on 'Gandhi' (1982)?

source.notes: Won alongside John Mollo.

questions.q: Which classic 1960 film, the longest-in-production Bollywood epic of its era, starred Dilip Kumar and Madhubala?

source.notes: K. Asif directed; over a decade in production.

questions.q: The 1975 'curry western' that is routinely cited as the greatest Hindi film of all time is called what?

source.notes: Directed by Ramesh Sippy; stars Bachchan and Dharmendra.
